content.full_text:
RAVE MENTA
CII Al R MAN
FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
June 4,
1996
Myrna Santos
City Councilmember
City of Parlier
Post Office Box
189
Parlier, California
93648—0189
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96-l67
Dear Ms.
Santos:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”)
1 as they relate to your duties as city councilmember for
the City of Parlier.
QUESTION
May you participate in the decision of whether or not to
approve a permanent attachment built next to the house in which
you and your parents reside?
CONCLUSION
Yes,
you may participate in the city council’s decision to
approve a permanent attachment to your parents’ house.
EACTS
You are a city councilmember in the City of Parlier.
You
live with your parents,
but you do not own the property.
All of
the bills,
including the mortgage,
are in the names of your
parents.
Recently,
your father removed the portable storage next
to the house in which you and your parents live and had a
permanent one built in the same location.
Since then,
your father
has been notified that since the attachment is permanent it needs
to be six feet away from the home.
Your father was given two
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections
18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STkir. Suiw 800, SACRAMENTO, CA[lFoINIA 95814
(916) 322566()


File No.
A—96—l67
Page
2
choices by the City of Parlier Code Enforcer:
remove the
attachment or take it to the city council for approval.
Your
father plans to take the question to the city council.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100
of the Act prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has
a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has
a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth one
thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public official
has
a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand
dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income,
other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status,
aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or.more in value
provided to,
received by or promised to the public
official within
12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is
a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or agent for
a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating two hundred
fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by,
or promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
2
(Section 87103.)
You may be disqualified if a decision will result in your
personal expenses,
income,
assets,
or liabilities,
or those of
your immediate family,
increasing or decreasing by at least $250.
(Regulation 18702.l(a)(4).)
“Immediate family” is defined in
2
The gift limit has
now been raised to $280.
(Section 87103(e);
Regulation 18940.1.)


File No.
A—96--l67
Page
3
Section 82029 as the official’s spouse and dependent children.
Because a public official’s parents are not members of the
official’s immediate family for purposes of the Act,
the familial
relationship between you and your parents does not,
by itself,
create a conflict of interest for you.
The facts you have provided indicate that you do not have
real property,
a business entity or source of income which will be
affected by the decision.
(Section 87103(a),
(b)
and (c).)
3
Accordingly,
you may participate in the decision to approve the
permanent attachment so long as the decision will not affect your
personal expenses by $250.
If you have any further questions,
please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
/
77 /
/‘ -
/
/21 /
K
By:
Liane Randolph
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:LR:ak
Even if you have received gifts from your parents,
those gifts
would not be disqualifying because gifts from an official’s
parents are not considered gifts for the purposes of the Act.
(Section 82028(b) (3).)
